reasons: Minotaur can create the control window that Odette needs to channel large AoE damage.
Odette benefits when Minotaur keeps enemies grouped or slowed during the teamfight setup.

proof.summary: Minotaur controls enemies in a grouped fight, while Odette follows with AoE damage during the control window.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur engages after Odette is close enough to follow.
Odette saves her ultimate until enemies are controlled.
failureCases: Odette is interrupted before the damage completes.
Minotaur catches enemies too far from Odette.

reasons: Pharsa can follow Minotaur's engage from safer distance with long-range damage.
Minotaur's frontline control gives Pharsa more time to cast from outside the center of the fight.

proof.summary: Minotaur creates the frontline control, while Pharsa punishes controlled enemies from range.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur engages inside Pharsa's damage range.
Pharsa is not forced to reposition before the follow-up.
failureCases: Enemy divers force Pharsa away before she can cast.

reasons: Minotaur can control enemies so Claude has a safer window to enter with AoE damage.
Claude benefits when Minotaur absorbs pressure before Claude commits into the fight.

proof.summary: Minotaur absorbs pressure and controls enemies, while Claude follows with AoE damage after the engage starts.
worksBestWhen: Claude waits for Minotaur's control before entering.
Minotaur forces enemies to spend key control before Claude commits.
failureCases: Claude enters before Minotaur creates space.
Enemy control interrupts Claude during his damage window.

reasons: Minotaur's frontline and control let Bane follow with AoE damage around objectives and grouped fights.
Bane benefits when Minotaur stabilizes the fight before Bane commits damage.

proof.summary: Minotaur starts or stabilizes the fight, while Bane adds AoE damage after enemies are controlled.
worksBestWhen: Bane stays close enough to follow Minotaur's engage.
The fight happens around an objective or choke area.
failureCases: Bane is zoned away before the follow-up.
Minotaur catches only targets Bane cannot safely damage.

reasons: Xavier can follow Minotaur's control from long range and add teamfight damage without standing inside the engage.
Minotaur gives Xavier a safer frontline while Xavier contributes skill damage from distance.

proof.summary: Minotaur creates a controlled frontline, while Xavier follows with long-range skill damage from safer positioning.
worksBestWhen: Xavier has line of sight and range when Minotaur engages.
Minotaur starts fights where enemies cannot easily spread out.
failureCases: Xavier is forced away before the engage.
Minotaur's control does not hold enemies long enough.
